scaled_oracle_cases.description: Replay of the BAR calculation with default parameters using synthetic data generated with seed 42, producing zero free energy difference and zero uncertainty.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify BAR works with Gaussian symmetric distributions and equal sample sizes.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The free energy difference should be 0.0 kT with uncertainty 0.0 kT.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Incorrect BAR implementation may produce non-zero values or fail to converge.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
result = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {result['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {result['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
covered_requirements: Enhance verification by adding two oracle cases with varied work distributions and parameter settings, testing asymmetric sample sizes and non-Gaussian inputs.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: BAR with asymmetric sample sizes (w_F=1000, w_R=5000) and seed 123 to test robustness to unbalanced data.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify BAR works with highly unbalanced sample sizes.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The Delta_f and dDelta_f should be finite and reasonable; the algorithm should not fail due to ratio of sizes.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Seed case (equal sizes) would not detect errors where BAR incorrectly handles asymmetric samples (e.g., ignoring sample size ratio).
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
result = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {result['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {result['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
covered_requirements: Enhance verification by adding two oracle cases with varied work distributions and parameter settings, testing asymmetric sample sizes and non-Gaussian inputs.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: BAR with exponentially distributed work values (non-Gaussian) to test robustness to heavy tails.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify BAR works with non-Gaussian (exponential) work distributions.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The algorithm should converge and produce valid Delta_f and dDelta_f, even when distributions are asymmetric and have tails.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Seed case (Gaussian) would not catch failures due to extreme values or poor overlap in exponential distributions.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
result = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {result['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {result['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
covered_requirements: Enhance verification by adding two oracle cases with varied work distributions and parameter settings, testing asymmetric sample sizes and non-Gaussian inputs.
